skills.notes: Cut ~2.3k lines of generic 'Nielsen heuristics list', 'Disney 12 principles', 'What is RICE', 'What is cohort analysis'. Kept non-obvious tradeoffs: state-management by data lifecycle, framework selection by context, WHEN to pick which motion pattern, contrast/focus/keyboard gotchas that bite in production. Role-play framing (`ROLE: Senior Frontend Engineer & UI/UX Designer`, `EXPERIENCE: 10+ years`) fully removed. All 14 source sections distilled into Decision Framework + Anti-Patterns + 5 references. Heavy reference usage is mandatory given source size; SKILL.md stays 131 lines.

Removed 2.8k lines of PRD-in-full-example, three giant persona cards, RICE arithmetic worked through twice, competitive matrix for a fictional productivity app. Kept: PM/eng decision boundary (the load-bearing PM concept), non-obvious framework-selection tradeoffs, metric-gaming warnings, A/B testing pitfalls, how-to-say-no scripts. Anti-patterns section has 12 'NEVER' lines with reasoning. Source's role-play framing (`ROLE: Product/Program Manager`, `EXPERIENCE: 8+ years`) deleted. References include full PRD templates since those remain useful but don't belong in core.

Largest source (2926 lines) compressed hardest. Cut giant Cypress/Playwright/k6/pytest/Jest code blocks (Claude knows these tools); kept WHEN to pick which, what to actually look for. Removed pyramid ASCII art (was in source); replaced with table + risk-shifted explanation. Anti-patterns NEVER list is 17 items — all non-obvious production-gotcha stuff (JWT none-alg, bcrypt vs SHA, constant-time compare, reachability over CVSS score). Security methodologies reference is the densest (262 lines) because OWASP Top 10, auth/session/crypto/header checklists are high-signal. Compliance reference includes cross-framework mapping table — useful for multi-framework orgs. 4 references instead of requested 5 (pentest + security-methodologies merged naturally since pentest methodology is inside sec methodology scope).
